entry.title: OData API request not working when Datasphere is set with SSO
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I have an OData URL that I use to get data from my Analytical Model in Datasphere:

the URL is:  https://tcodataspherefree.eu10.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/TCO_DATA_MODEL/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering/Landscape_daily_cost_capacity_metering?$top=10

It works just fine if I use it in my browser after I sign in with SSO.

But when I want to call it through a python code, after setting up OAuth on Datasphere, I am not getting any data back from the URL. I assume it is because the SSO is set.

Is there any way to get around that?

Many thanks

Sam

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to export commodity codes from a list...
entry.content: Go to MM60 insert your materials and run the list, change layout and insert Comm. code column to view the commodity codes

entry.title: SD: Trigger billing after GI posting
entry.content:
Hello gurus,

 

we would like to automatically trigger creation of a bill when GI is posted for the delivery.

 

I.e. user creates sales order, creates delivery and posts the GI for the delivery. Now the bill for that delivery is to be created automatically.

 

It seems, SAP CRM does have a feature for this, but is it possible in ECC as well?

 

Thanks
Jens

entry.title: Top 10 from the SAP Cloud ALM October 2024 Update
entry.content:
Top 10 Highlights from the SAP Cloud ALM October 2024 Update

SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve with new features that enhance project management, monitoring, and traceability. Here are the top 10 highlights from the latest October 2024 update:

1. Mass Assignment of Project Tasks to Quality Gates

The new mass assignment feature allows users to assign multiple project tasks to quality gates effortlessly. This enhancement is particularly useful for managing large-scale projects efficiently.

 

2. Enhanced Overview Cards

Users can perform additional actions directly from the cards on the Home page. These actions include:

  • Editing configuration
  • Navigating to monitoring details
  • Navigating to alerting details

This makes accessing crucial information quicker and easier.

3. Visual Test Execution and Traceability Enhancements

The test execution popover now shows all occurrences of a test case assigned to different test plans. This improvement extends to the Requirement, Solution Process, and User Story Traceability apps, offering comprehensive visibility into test statuses.

4. Critical Subscription Marking

Subscriptions in external API Management can now be marked as "Critical," enabling a self-monitoring capability that proactively identifies and addresses potential issues, enhancing system reliability.

5. Reference Links in Test Cases

Users can add links to external documents and web pages when preparing manual and automated test cases. This feature helps testers access supporting documents like process descriptions and learning materials directly.

6. Comment Integration on Overview Page

A COMMENTS column has been added to the Overview page, allowing users to view the latest comments on issues and standalone actions. This improves communication transparency and issue management efficiency.

7. Quality Gate Status Validation

The status of a quality gate can only be set to Accepted or Conditionally Accepted if the assigned checklist items have valid statuses. Once set, these statuses cannot be changed, ensuring the integrity of the review process.

8. Direct Navigation from Test Case to Requirement Traceability App

Users can now navigate from the test case section in the detail view of a requirement directly to the Requirement Traceability app. This seamless transition enhances traceability and tracking of test execution statuses.

9. Process Hierarchy Nodes Assignment

Test cases can now be assigned to specific process hierarchy nodes, allowing for detailed pinpointing within the process structure and precise process-related test case management.

10. Custom Time Zone for Notifications

Users can specify the time zone for email and chat notifications, customizing the timestamp to align with their local time zone. This personalization ensures more accurate reporting and alerts.

These top 10 features in the October 2024 SAP Cloud ALM update are designed to streamline operations, improve management efficiency, and enhance user experience. For more detailed information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

Stay tuned for more exciting updates as SAP continues to innovate and improve the #Cloud ALM experience

entry.title: Re: Send SAPscript form PDF from output type message to Business Network
entry.content:
Hi ,
How  can we send the attachment directly whenever we save the PO in me21n,me22n,me23n ? I am getting pdf in ariba network when I send it through me9f as own transaction. I need to send it immediately . thanks in advance for help

entry.title: Workflow email notifications being sent in the wrong language
entry.content:
Hello experts

I am configuring the email notifications so the employees can receive them in Portuguese. Under Email Notifications Template Settings I choose the desire notification (Workflow action Approval Notification, for example) and I make sure that the translation to Portuguese is there. 

Then, with the system in Portuguese, the email notification continues to be sent in English. 

Do you know if I am missing some steps on the configuration or what could be the reason of this situation?

Thanks in advance

Has anyone else noticing same issue?

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to Retrigger Output Parameter Determination Autom...
entry.content: Is there a way to automatically re-trigger Outputs for On-Premise systems? I think that's what "ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE"" is supposed to do, but it doesn't.

entry.title: Re: Results Analysis Method 7, POC Method Based On Project Progress Value Determination
entry.content: For estimation of PoC, T-code CJ20N, tab Progress, click on progress version, then button estimated values.

entry.title: Creating a personal development environment #3 - Vim keybindings
entry.content:
1. #1 - Why Neovim?
  2. #2 - Miscellaneous, status line, and color scheme
  3. #3 - Vim keybindings (this blog post)

Building on top of the previous two blog posts, this blog post will focus learning Vim keybindings, which is very central for using any Vim-based editor, let alone be productive with it.

Before we get started with the keybindings though, let's do a quick recap of what my Neovim configuration, located at ~/.config/nvim/, looked like a this point:

- lua/
  - plugins/
    - filetree.lua
    - theme.lua
  - misc.lua
  - statusline.lua
- init.lua
- lazy-lock.json

What I did so far:

  • configured lazy.nvim as a plugin manager
  • added nvim-tree as a file explorer
  • customized the status line
  • added a nice color scheme
  • other miscellaneous settings

Now while doing all of this, we implicitly already talked about keybindings. After all, Neovim is a keyboard-first editor. You shouldn't really be using the mouse at all (although you can if you want to), which is why you have to use keybindings. However, we only really talked about custom keybindings - the ones that relate to a plugin and where you define the shortcut and the associated operation. But the most important keybindings are the ones that are built into Neovim itself, and since Neovim is a fork of Vim (did I already mention that? 🤔), we can simply refer to them as Vim keybindings (also when googling 😉). These are the ones you will use the most for moving around, switching modes, editing text, and so on. Vim keybindings can seem quite odd at first (from a modern perspective), but they are incredibly powerful and well though-out.

Regarding all my custom keybindings I mentioned that I like to group my keybindings by the category they fall into (in my understanding) and prefix them with the corresponding letter. For example, all keybinding related to the file system start with the letter f, like fe for opening the file explorer. Another example are all bindings related to the language server start with the letter l, like ld for "go to definition". This system helps me remembering them all. It somewhat different to what other people mostly do, which is prefixing all keybindings with a defined leader key, for example space.

Vim keybindings

I will not even try to list or explain the structure of all Vim keybindings in this blog post - others can do that better. I will also not extensively talk about modes, I only really use normal, insert, and visual mode. But I can give you an insight into the keybindings I use on a daily basis and how I personally make sense of them. Hopefully this helps you prioritize which keybindings to learn first and how to remember them. The keybindings listed here work in normal and visual mode, but not in insert mode, where your keystrokes will obviously be interpreted as text input.

For navigating in a file, I use the following keybindings:

  • G: Go to the end of the file
  • gg: Go to the beginning of the file
  • $: Go to the end of the line
  • 0: Go to the beginning of the line
  • e: Move to the end of the current word
  • b: Move to the beginning of the current word

And I do have to confess I am one of the few Vim users who actually uses the arrow keys to move around (I know, I know), instead of h, j, k, and l. I have to reasons for that: First of all, I just couldn't get used to them. Second of all, I often find myself wanting to navigate around in insert mode, and the arrow keys are just more convenient in that situation than switching modes.

For editing text, I use the following keybindings:

  • i: Enter insert mode
  • a: Enter insert mode after the current cursor position (one character further right than with i)
  • A: Enter insert mode at the end of the line
  • u: Undo the last change
  • Ctrl-r: Redo the last change
  • dw: Delete the current word
  • dd: Delete the current line
  • D: Delete from the current cursor position to the end of the line

For selecting text and copy-pasta magic, I use the following keybindings:

  • v: Enter visual mode
  • V: Enter visual line mode (visually select whole line)
  • y: Yank (copy) the selected text (in visual mode)
  • d: Delete the selected text (in visual mode)
  • c: Change the selected text (in visual mode, delete and enter insert mode)
  • p: Paste the yanked text after the cursor

With this set of keybindings (that barely scratches the surface of what's possible) you can actually accomplish a lot of things, especially when combining them. For example, for navigating to the end of the file and going into insert mode at the end of the last line, you can simply use GA. There are probably more efficient ways to get certain things done, but this "minimalistic" set of keybindings works well for me and most importantly, I can remember them. This is just the start and there is a lot more to learn, but that takes time and practice. I will try and add more keybindings to my repertoire as I go along. It's a fun journey.

And that's it for this blog post. Let's talk about the language server and how you can tailor it to your liking next time - arguably the most important and coolest part of a modern development environment.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
Dear @erika_buson thanks a lot for this really useful blog.

In spain i dont see the supported scenario for DRC cloud edition. We are facing in the upcoming year (probably) a new tax regulation in B2B (similar to the Poland scenario)

Supported Business Scenarios | SAP Help Portal

The thing is, we have clients with version ERP 6.0 eph7 no Unicode. And the client is trying to anticipate is they are going to need the integration suite or the DRC cloud edition will be enough to fullfill the needs. Are you aware of this information?

 

Kind regards.

Have you checked KBA 3432140: DI_When using an Add-on you get an unhandled COMException

Please try solution mentioned in this.

Kind Regards
Nidhi

Is this default behavior or I am missing something, tried all the possible combinations and the problem appears only when we download it as pdf.

Your inputs are highly appreciated, thanks.

Regards,
Vijey

entry.title: Re: Ensure that you have a database available in space 'd...
entry.content: I have the same issue, I didn't find any way to solve it

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Cloud or On-Premise? Not an “either or” option, but a str
entry.content:
@Jonathan10  the link above has the requirements/scenarios already released. As you mentioned, B2B e-invoices in Spain are future roadmap (there are also rumours of delays, no dates are confirmed). For upcoming regulatory changes, you can refer to regulatory change manager.

Specifically for Spain B2B e-invoices, this is the link and you can refer to the corresponding product impacts tab. You can click/visualise the entry for SAP ERP and you can find the info you are looking for.

We also have a webinar replay, EN and ES. 

Regarding minimum version, you can refer to this page for framework level.

Of course, country-specific pre-requisites can change until development is completed.

Thank you. Erika

entry.title: Re: Professional Service for billing the product P003 is...
entry.content: Hi David, many thanks for your feedback. I'll use P002 for usage based billing.

entry.title: Start learning to develop extensions for SAP Business One, Web client for customer implementations
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Professional Request for Business Users learning journey.


Overview
This learning journey is for developers who want to learn how to develop extensions for SAP Business One, Web client for customer implementations.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, developers will be able to: 

  • Exploit the options for extending SAP Business One, Web client.     
  • Leverage the SAP Business One Service Layer OData API. 
  • Develop, package, and deploy UI API Extensions for SAP Business One, Web client to customize system views to suit specific business needs. 
  • Build and deploy side-by-side Fiori applications with SAP BTP.

Prerequisites

  • Basic knowledge of SAP Business One and the Web Client. 
  • Recommended learning journey: Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client
  • Basic knowledge of OData and REST API.
  • Basic knowledge of Microsoft Visual Studio Code and JavaScript (Node.js).
  • Basic knowledge of cloud development concepts. 
Record of Achievement
Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: abap2UI5 - (1) Introduction: Developing UI5 Apps Purely in ABAP
entry.content:
HI @Octav ,

Check out the documentation for all the information on how to get started:
https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/

entry.title: Re: Barcode Scanning function using ABAP
entry.content: Find all information about barcode scanning with abap2UI5: https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/development/barcodes.html

entry.title: Re: Upgrade : SAPKB70208
entry.content:
Hello,

the warning generated by REV-TRAC tool seems not to be correct if both systems are already on higher release SAP_BASIS 731 and on the same SP level.

SAP_BASIS 731 initial delivery is equivalent with SAP_BASIS 702 SP8, meaning, all objects were delivered in 731 with the same development level as 702 SP8.

It seems the tool compares the installed SPs where the object was delivered and SP8 object list cannot be found on the target system as the object was delivered via export DVDs of release SAP_BASIS 731.

Please contact REV-TRAC and ask for a fix.

Best regards,

Laszlo

entry.title: Re: 403 Forbidden Error when trying to access to the tile...
entry.content: abap2UI5 is successfully tested with S/4 Public Cloud. Find all information about the installation here: https://abap2ui5.github.io/docs/configuration/s4_public_cloud.html

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @prasanna_korada2 take a look at DAC modeling using a hierarchy: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/0afeeed6b56a40f5a02581032ec39420.html?locale=en-US. You cannot use Hierarchy with Directory for this, so you might need to dumb down your hierarchy to fit the more basic feature set of a view with semantic usage "hierarchy". This blog might help you with that: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/build-an-sap-datasphere-parent-child-hierarchy-based-on-s-4hana-hierarchies/ba-p/13497286

entry.content:
Hi

how we can send email notification via My inbox Fiorii App ?

entry.title: Re: System check for Orphaned Client Dependent Data
entry.content: It’s interesting how even incomplete client deletion jobs from the past can linger and cause issues later on, especially in a large system environment. Running checks like SCC3 and database analysis through DB02, as well as collaborating with the DBA team, could really help catch these potential pitfalls EdFinancial

entry.title: Re: End to End Master data replication from SAP S/4 HANA to SAP Ariba using IaaS (Cloud Integration

sorry for the late reply. The above presented solution should also work in SAPGUI screens. If not I would suggest to create a ticket, so that the root cause can be investigated and fixed.

Karsten

entry.title: Re: How to send SAP data to Advanced Event Mesh in 15 minutes, code-less
entry.content:
I'm glad to hear the post helped @loather!
Yes, you can specify constants in your join by using the WHERE condition inside the Payload Designer.
Here's the documentation for more details on how to set up this kind of filtering in Payload Designer: Payload Designer Documentation.

entry.title: Re: Call API returns code 500

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Change Text interpretation runtime is...
entry.content:
Always try it in CR Designer to see if it works, if it doesn't work in CRD it won't work in code.

As soon as you set the text to HTML it automatically left justifies.

This is by design...

entry.title: Re: CPI: Messages are failing due to mapping issue and co...
entry.content:
Hi Aamir,

yes i am referring to alert configuration and also log error with payload.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: SD: Trigger billing after GI posting
entry.content:
You can have a background Job for creating Invoice that is executed frequently. 

The frequency can be every 15 minutes.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Event Processing in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise system
entry.content:
Thanks, Erik. I know to use the standard event. But my case it is my own event on some own created. On not a standard business object. But I might beable to get in event monitor. 

Br. 

Anna

entry.title: Re: SAP Personas , script OnEnter with unwanted "enter" function - HUPAST
entry.content:
Good morning, Emmanuel.

If I understand correctly you are scanning the EAN into the "material/handling unit" field and setting a value to the quantity field? I'm curious to know more about what you are doing because I also have a HUPAST flavor and am on Personas SP18 / ECC6 EHP7.

Please check if your barcode scanner is configured to have a carriage return on scan. This might be how the onEnter event is being triggered.

Kindly,

Cristin

entry.title: Re: Call API returns code 500
entry.content: @amontella96 You was right that I did not transfer one requirement to service. See Ryan-Crosby's answer. Thank you for your help

entry.title: Re: Professional Service for billing the product P003 is...
entry.content:
Hi David,

many thanks for your feedback. I'll use P002 for usage based billing.

Within the Billing item details tab (APP Customer Project) I could not add the 

specific monthly volume = 500 (for Month 1) and calculated with sales price 1,15 €

per unit (=Volume).

View Monthly billing plan: In the billing plan I could not add the monthly Volume

of 500 for the first months; next billing period it could be 620 .....

There is no field to add the Volume (=500) and to see the revenue calculation

with 1,15 € per unit  =  575 € for preliminary billing:

David@: I hope you could support 

br, Michael

entry.title: Grouping all SAP TM Blogs which I have created into to this one to centralized it.
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I am grouping all Blogs which I have created into to this one to centralized it.

Automatically FU rebuilding Planning
https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/automatically-fu-rebuilding-planning/ba-p/13572958 

Automatic Freight Unit Planning

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/freight-cost-allocation-in-sap-tm-integration-with-financial-area-of-sap/ba-p/13575303

Analytics KPI’s in Transportation Management

How to Check PPF (Output Management) technically In Transportation Management

How to enable logs in SAP TM

SAP TM Integration with Last Mile Distribution

Use of Combination Resources in SAP TM

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-tm-auditing-tm-vs-advanced-tm/ba-p/13573605

Freight Cost Allocation in SAP TM Integration with Financial area of SAP.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-tm-strategy-to-re-determined-organizational-structure/ba-p/13930048

Regards
Rogerio

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to customize the table subtitle?
entry.content:
@cham_xu 

You cannot. But you can use a text field overlayed on top of table to show what you need and switch off the table subtitle.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Feasibility of Attaching Document in Mail Notification Alerts in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I hope you are doing well.

One of my customers has this following ask regarding the feasibility of attaching documents to mail notifications triggered by alerts in SuccessFactors. Specifically, I would like to understand whether it is possible to include actual document attachments in these email alerts or if only document links can be embedded in the notification. Could you please confirm if attaching documents is supported, and if so, provide any relevant guidelines or configurations to enable this feature? Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Error Encountered During Status change for Standard C...
entry.content:
@Vera_Jiang, you mean you can mark an answer as accepted on behalf of the original person who posted the question?.... 😧

That is a bad precedence. The OP should be the only person who can accept answers, to make sure it really solves their issue. If they don't come back, so be it, the question will remain unanswered.  

Talk about abusive power!

entry.title: Change 'Requisition Processing State' and 'Tracking Number' for PR via BAPI_PR_CHANGE
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to change the Requisition Processing State' and 'Tracking Number' for PR via Integration. Can you please let me know what values to pass into the BAPI_PR_CHANGE to get this changed for a PR ?

Can we use BAPI_PR_CHANGE or is there any other BAPI to achieve this ?

adding the screenshots for reference.

 

Help greatly appreciated.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Analytical and Relational OData APIs
entry.content: Do you know how much data is retrieved from a view on every api call? Is there some kind of limitation to the amount of data or rows? In case your view has more than 1000 000 rows, how it will be retrieved.

entry.title: Re: Poor performance in API API_SALES_ORDER_SRV GET method - order reading
entry.content: solution:

entry.title: Re: Poor performance in API API_SALES_ORDER_SRV GET metho...
entry.content:
Switch Off Language-Specific Sorting to Improve Performance

SADL runtime uses language-specific alphabetical sorting as the default sort strategy. If sort criteria are defined, the records in the result are sorted according to the alphabet of current user's language.

Prerequisites

This feature is available since SAP NetWeaver 7.40 SP08.

Context

The disadvantage of using language-specific sorting is that the database might need much more time to execute the query, even with factor 10 or more. To protect performance, you can switch the language-specific sorting off, if it is not needed. It is recommended that you use language-specific sorting only if:

  • $orderby is defined on a text field or
  • $filter with operators 'gt', 'ge', 'lt' or 'le' is defined on a text field.

The text field is a database field that contains language-specific texts. In all other cases you can switch the language-specific sorting off by calling the following method:

io_query_options->set_locale_disabled( abap_true ).

entry.title: Re: Error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet C...
entry.content: @Dev_2000 Where do you see a connection timeout? I only see a connection broken.

entry.title: Data issue in Standard CDS view 2CCFIFINSTMTKPI
entry.content:
Hi,

We have created dashboard on the standard CDS view 2CCFIFINSTMTKPI in SAC. We are facing a strange issue where EBITDA is not matching in 2 charts. The global currency is showing same as AUD.

Left chart- EBITDA (EBITDAAmountInGlobCrcy) is showing EBITDA value

Right chart-Amount in Global Currency (AmountInGlobalCurrency) is showing values for G/L account hierarchy, here we have selected EBITDA in the filter. So now, the chart show EBITDA value.

Ideally, these 2 charts should match with each other. The left chart value is matching with S/4.

entry.title: CDS View for Planned cost and revenue.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW DTP dynamic filter multiple fields
entry.content:
Hi

 

Check the Note:

1675245 - P29:DTP:Sel. routine behavior different than in InfoPackage

Theoretically you can have access to other fields filters in a filter routine when the RSBKADMIN parameter DETERMINE_ROUTINES_LIKE_IPAK is set.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: SD: Trigger billing after GI posting
entry.content:
Hello @jens_feldkamp1 

It is possible to create a billing document automatically.

Please go to the VF06 transaction code and schedule a background job according to your requirements, as suggested by @jignesh_mehta3 

Best Regards
Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Error : SOAP:1023 SRT: Processing error in Internet C...
entry.content: Check with Network Admin (yours and provider of service) for problem related to https or firewall, and read a KBA such as 2149132 - ICM performance checks

entry.title: Re: Change 'Requisition Processing State' and 'Tracking Number' for PR via BAPI_PR_CHANGE
entry.content: Unless someone knows it from their head your best shot at finding a solution is to click into the field, hit F1 and then the wrench icon for the technical information. This shows you the name of the variable for the field. Usually a structure and not a transparent table (if the tech info says table you are golden because now you just need to open that table in SE16N and look at the values). In case it's a structure you may go to SE11 and open the structure, select the field and click on "Where used" in the menu bar. Then find the program that uses the field and look around in the code to find hints where the data is pulled. If you are successfull at locating the part of the code accessing a table you proceed as if tech info showed you a table. GL on that!

partner -> saprouter ->  RFC gateway

entry.title: Changing output file name when printing from Crystal to PDF using RAS Controller
entry.content: We are trying to set the output file name programmatically when printing to PDF from Crystal Viewer so the user will bypass the Save As window. We can do it using crwPrinterSettings:PrintFileName if RAS Controller is off for the document but crwPrintOptions:PrintFileName is not valid. What is the valid syntax when using RAS controller?

entry.title: Re: Uploading Document in SAP Marketing Cloud forms
entry.content: Hey KunalBansal, As per my knowledge, SAP Marketing Cloud does not support a direct document upload in forms. A common workaround is to use SAP Document Management Service (DMS) to store documents, and then share folder link with each contact automatically. Alternatively, you could consider building a custom extension in SAP BTP for enhanced document handling.

entry.title: Access Control option for workspaces in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition
entry.content:
As organisations grow and diversify, so does the need for flexible, secure, and tailored access control within collaboration platforms.

SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition addresses this need with its advanced workspace privacy and access options, tailored to meet a different range of business requirements. This post dives into how these features support unique access configurations, offering business leaders, employees, and external end users the control they need over collaborative workspaces.

Public vs. Private Workspaces: The Core Options

At the foundation of SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition are two main workspace types: Public and Private.

  • Public Workspaces: Ideal for general information accessible by all employees, such as HR policies, product updates, or company news. These spaces are searchable across the SAP Build Work Zone site, and ensure employees can easily locate essential resources.
  • Private Workspaces: Designed for limited access, these workspaces are only available to a selected group of members. Employees must be invited or added via a user list, with non-members unable to search or view the content, which helps ensure sensitive information stays within specified groups (e.g., finance teams, regional HR policies).

Worth mentioning, a user can also add private content in a public space, if he/she wants to share it with a smaller audience. This is done by selecting New Private Folder from the create content dropdown (as shown in the following screenshot)

 

External User Access

 In certain cases, companies may want to grant external users - such as suppliers, consultants, or partners - access to specific workspaces without exposing the entire SAP Build Work Zone site. By selecting an external access option (available for both Public and Private workspaces), workspace admins can securely share essential information or collaborate with users outside the organisation.

Workspaces with external access are marked as “External” to alert members to potential privacy considerations, helping them avoid sharing confidential information (see screenshot below).

Furthermore, as a workspace creator, you can also control whether other members can join the workspace and whether they can invite others without requiring your approval as the workspace admin. (See the screenshot below).

Customisable Collaboration Levels and Task Policy Management

Beyond access settings, SAP Build Work Zone provides collaboration level options tailored to the workspace's purpose and members’ needs:

  • Read-Only: Members can view content without contributing.-
  • Limited Collaboration: Members can interact with limited editing permissions.
  • Full Collaboration: Offers the highest level of engagement, allowing members to actively participate in workspace activities.

For workspaces with Limited or Full collaboration, Task Policy editing options further define control over task updates. Workspace administrators can decide if tasks should be editable by all members, limited to assignees and admins, or strictly restricted to creators and admins. This setup helps creating transparency and define task accountability according to the Workspace’s requirements (see screenshot below).

Upload and Approval Policies

Content-sharing policies are critical for managing sensitive information. Workspace admins have options to enforce upload and approval policies, which determine whether content (like PDFs, videos, or posts) can be shared freely by members or if admin review is required (see screenshot). This ensures that only authorised information appears in the workspace, particularly in cases where compliance or quality control is essential.

Non-Member Participation and Access Policies

Non-member participation provide additional layers of security and flexibility:

Collaboration Level: Admins can allow non-members to view content or restrict all collaborative tools and member details. For cases requiring transparency across a department or broader audience, enabling non-member access can facilitate information sharing without compromising security.

Access Policies: Some workspaces may require restricted access for specific employee subgroups (e.g., HR or Sales), instead of granting access to the full organisation. These access policies allow granular control of visibility and participation, ensuring the workspace meets unique business requirements.

These flexible customisations are designed to meet diverse organisational needs, whether that’s ensuring company-wide access to HR updates or creating secure, department-specific workspaces for internal projects or partnerships with external collaborators.

By leveraging these options, your organisation can optimise SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition to match your unique operational needs, balancing open collaboration with tailored, role-based access, thus protecting sensitive information and at the same time promoting effective teamwork.

SUMMARY

Decision Table: 

What I want to achieve?Which configurations I need:
A Workspace visible to all employees. Each employee could join and invite other members.

Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to join this workspace
A workspace visible to all employees, to but as a workspace admin I want to control who has access and I want to be the only one inviting members

Create a Public Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace which is visible to all employees AND external users (prior invitation)Create a Public Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. As a workspace admin, I want to be the only only one who can invite users (users cannot request to join).

Create a Private Workspace + leave unselected all other checkboxes.

A workspace visible only to members and accessible via invitation. Members can invite other users and users are able to request to join the workspace.

Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:

  • Allow all members to invite users,
  • Allow users to request to join this workspace
A workspace which is visible to only to member BUT ALSO to some external users (prior invitation)Create a Private Workspace + select checkbox:
  • External users can access this workspace and be invited as members
A Workspace (private or public) to share information, in which members can only view and download resources.After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Read Only.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take some actions on existing content such as view, comment, download. They can also handle tasks but not create ones.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Limited.

Remember to select task policy according to needs:

  • Tasks are editable by creators and workspace admins. Status can be updated by task assignee.
  • Tasks are editable by assignees, creators, and workspace admins. Status can be updated by all members.
  • Task details and status can be updated by all members.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy.

A Workspace (private or public)  in which members can collaborate, and take all actions on content such as view, comment, download, edit, rename and delete. They can also create and handle tasks.

All members can upload content, but as an admin you want to approve content sharing.

After creating the Workspace, click next and Select collaboration Level Full + select checkbox Upload Policy and select checkbox Approval Policy.

In a public or Private space, you want to share some content visible only to a subset of specific users.

Click on the hamburger menu on your Workpage, select Content from the available options, click on the Create dropdown menu and select New Private Folder.

entry.title: Re: Verify General Journal Entries - Configuration Steps in S/4HANA 1909
entry.content: Thanks @Subhrangsu for this amazing & detailed document on the JV workflow. Appreciate your hard work and knowledge on this. Thanks for sharing

entry.title: Re: Blocked Transactions SAP ME / HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

Can't be certain, but they look like queries from standard ME.

1) could be SFC Qty Adjust, Split, or release of additional qty for example.

2) and 3) are for updating Resource_Time_log / Resource_Time_Member whenever an SFC is placed in work or removed from work (Start /Signoff  / Complete).Check that Track Resource Time system rule is only enabled on resources where it is needed, and check that you don't have old SFCs left abandoned but active at a resource, and that old RTL/RTM records are getting archived.

 

Regards,

Andrew.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Regarding the "The service metadata is invalid" error...
entry.content: I actually managed to get it working last week by using the npm package "yo" which allows the creation of UIs inside the cmd. I have not tested the proposed solution of yours, but that could actually be the case as I also used a ".env" file for database credentials. Really hope this gets fixed soon tho as it's still easier to use the Fiori Generator for the given task ref @cothe

entry.title: Re: ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI to create inforecord wit...
entry.content:
Hi,

In case it helps I got my problem solved, I was doing the right coding as the sample provided by SAP in 3070467 - ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI: Template for creating info record.

My fault was I was checking the inforecord on ME11 and depending if you enter the plant or not, you display a different inforecord, so my test coding was processing the inforecord without plant and I was checking the inforecord with plant. I realized while debugging and checking the reference table could be A017 (Plant specific) or A018.

entry.title: Guided Sourcing Project - Approval Task - Document Types

However I am unable to find any information on which documents it works for e.g. not power point, and to what extent. e.g. Word does work but is formatted really strangely. PDFs work great.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content: Looks great but why are there no updates to the contracts Module? When are we going to get "guided" contracts with the improved look and feel?

entry.title: Cycle Counting Physical Inventory in SAP WM at Bin Level
entry.content:
Introduction- In cycle counting inventory, you enter your material stocks physically at regular intervals within a fiscal year. By dividing materials into various classes (for example, A, B, C, D, and so on), you specify for those materials at which intervals or cycles the inventory is to be performed. This allows you to make an inventory fast-moving materials in your warehouse more often than slow-moving material.

Cycle counting is basically the process of continually validation the accuracy of the inventory in the warehouse by regularly counting a portion of the inventory on a daily or weekly basis. Every item in the warehouse is counted at lease several times in a year.

Cycle Counting can be performed at IM level as well as WM Level.

In WM  We can go for two types of Cycle Counting Physical Inventory

1- Cycle Counting at Bin Level

2- Cycle Counting at Quant Level 

In this blog I am going to write about Cycle Counting at Bin Level

WM Level Cycle Counting Configuration

  • ABC  indicator has to be maintained in Material Management

Navigation SPRO-Material Management-Inventory Management and Physical Inventory-Physical Inventory-Cycle Counting

Master Data Set UP at IM Level

Set Cycle Counting Indicator (M1BC)

Navigation Logistics – Material Management- Physical Inventory- Special Procedures-Cycle Counting – Set Cycle Counting Indicator

Click change CC Indicator

It can be checked in MM03

 

Cycle Counting Configuration in WM for Storage Type

Navigation SPRO-Logistic Execution- Warehouse Management – Activity – Physical Inventory -Define Types per storage type

Process Flow

Create Cycle Counting Document in WM

Transaction Code LX26

Count the inventory

Transaction code – LI11n

Input Counted quantity In put here lesser than actual

And save the count

Inventory Count Added

Clear difference in WM

Transaction LI20

Write Off the count

Clear difference at IM level

Transaction code LI21 or LX23

I am taking here LI21

After write off  Material document will be created

Check LX03

One pallet quantity was 0 so it disappeared

Other pallet quantity 1 and it was increased by 1 quantity so it is changed

After the entire physical inventory is complete for the fiscal year, you reset the inventory data for storage bins and storage quants using the RLREOLPQ report. In the next inventory period, you can then perform a new physical inventory for these storage bins.

Note- You should not reset the quant data, since the reference to the inventory date is then lost, and the system proposes too many bins for your next cycle counting inventory.

Reference - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/8f91c95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

Please share your feedback so I can improve it 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Release Date of BI Platform 4.3 SP05
entry.content:
Hi

Where can I find the release date of SAP BI Platform 4.3 SP05?

regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: SAC Data Action Advanced Formula - dynamic Average
entry.content: Hi CA_Priya， you first need to change the book behavior of your date dimension in modeler to allow data booking on period '000000' .

entry.title: SAP Thailand Localization Virtual Session 2024
entry.content:
Join us at the SAP Thailand Localization Virtual Session 2024 on November 20th, 2024!

During this two-hour event, we will provide updates on the most recent legal changes in Thailand and offer a comprehensive overview of the various SAP delivered localization functions, including those planned for future release.

Get invaluable knowledge about SAP localization solutions, which can speed up your digital business transformation on a global scale!

Agenda:
13:30 Localization update on SAP S/4HANA & SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
14:00 AI power Localization & Notification Integration Service for Social Media Apps
14:30 HCM Legal Compliance Update
15:15 Q&A

Speakers:
Zhao, Nelly, SAP (China) Co., Ltd.
Su, Alice, SAP (China) Co., Ltd.

*Session will be mainly presented in Thai. Only session at 14:00 will be presented in English by foreign speakers.

** You can join the session at the time of your interest topic.


Registration Link

entry.title: Re: AFO SAC Edition and Standard Edition installed simult...
entry.content: if your AO workbook is created with connection through SAC, it should be possible.

entry.title: Re: Task Document tab
entry.content: If you are going to log it as an influence/enhancement request and share the number, than we can also vote for it!

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture for integration?
entry.content:
Hi @stevang ... please allow me to quickly share a thought / comment.
I came to EA via the topic of integration.
In my opinion, the first step to successful integration is to clearly understand the role of the different components. Why they are needed, why are they there, which capabilities do they address, to which part of the process shall they contribute, what is the information required, ...?

This has been true for creating SAP reference architectures covering components around latest acquisitions, but even more in EA work for enterprises in the industry, that leverage and combine components of different software vendors and service providers. 

The SAP Enterprise Architecture Methodology invests in the seamless connection to solution architecture, data architecture, and other architecture domains. We want to ensure that practitioners benefit from this connection and consistent messaging along the implementation lifecycle. 
The SAP Enterprise Architecture aims to not only close the gap between Business and IT, but also between EA and implementation (e.g. ACTIVATE) and operations.

Hope that resonates.

entry.title: S4 Upgrade: TCI SAPK10601ACPS4CORE not available
entry.content:
Good Afternoon,

I am performing an Upgrade from S4 2021FPS02 to 2023FPS02.
I have an error in the phase REP_SIF_CHECKS/SUBMOD_S4H_CHECK_INIT/RUN_S4H_SIF_CHECK_INIT.
It requires SAP note 3304656.
This not implementation reports the following error:
The transport based correction (TCI) SAPK10601ACPS4CORE of SAP note 0003304656 is not available.

After reading Note 3308847, 2499947 or 3471138, I have tried to download the TCI SAPK10601ACPS4CORE by searching it on the SAP Support Portal.
As This returns 0 result, I would like to know if you would know I could download it?

Have a good day.

entry.title: Re: SD: Trigger billing after GI posting
entry.content: Hello and thanky, I fear I did not make myself clear: Billing is required to start immediatelly aufter posting the GI. A job unfortunatelly is not sufficient. Sorry Jens

entry.title: SAP MDK capabilities like charts for web and chat bot like UI layout for mobile
entry.content:
Hi All,

Few queries around sap MDK app development, please help me to find the answers.

Note:  I use BAS for development.

1. is it possible to build chat bot like UI layout(refer below sample design) using MDK, as its not free style UI5 app I am finding it difficult to bring up complex designs.

2. I am using MDK control 'Chart Content' but it only supports mobile, how to build charts for web too ? also 'Chart content' doesn't have pie chart ? 

3. I want to call an odata service entity and do some processing and setup some global variables to hold different data sets to use across different pages, is it possible in MDK ?

4. Do we have any portal where sample designs with source code are given to showcase SAP MDK's UI capabilities ? something like demokit for Fiori ? I found this https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-mdk-samples/tree/main/Showcase_Apps but I feel that there should be more design samples showcased somewhere else ?

@Jitendra_Kansal  @bill_froelich Plz throw some light here when you can.

entry.title: Re: Type ERROR:Cannot read properties of undefined (readi...
entry.content:
Your manifest.json looks okay. Does the cds view for the Order entityset have the proper analytical annotations? Here's a blog with examples.

To check your UI5 version, when running the app in FLP press Ctrl-Alt-Shift-P.
